chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Crl. Bail Appln No.S-686 of 2025 DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE. 1. For orders on M.A. No.4100/25 2. For orders on O/objection at flag-A. 3. For orders on M.A. No.4101/25 4. For hearing of bail application. 08.08.2025 Mr. Parmanand, Advocate alongwith applicant. ******** 1. Urgency allowed. 2. As to the office objection learned counsel states that same shall be complied well before obtaining copy of order. 3. Granted, subject to all just exceptions. 4. Through instant application, applicant Rohit Kumar son of Keerat Mal seeks Transitory-cum-protective bail in Crime No.62/2025 Police Station, NCCIA Rawalpindi Circle Province Punjab, under Sections 3, 4, 14, 16 & 26 of Prevention of Electronic Crimes Act, 2016 r/w 419, 420 & 109 of PPC. It is, inter alia, contended that applicant is innocent and has falsely been involved in this case by the FIA Police with malafide intention and ulterior motives. It is further contended that as soon as applicant came to know he tried to surrender himself before the concerned Court for pre-arrest bail but applicant has grave apprehension of his being arrest before reaching the same at the hands of concerned police hence, he has approached this Court for grant of protective bail. Be that as it may, without touching merits and demerits of the case and in order to enable applicant to appear before concerned Court, he is granted Transitory-cum-protective bail for a period of 10(Ten) days commencing from today, subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty thousand) and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court.

It is further contended that as soon as applicant came to know he tried to surrender himself before the concerned Court for pre-arrest bail but applicant has grave apprehension of his being arrest before reaching the same at the hands of concerned police hence, he has approached this Court for grant of protective bail. Be that as it may, without touching merits and demerits of the case and in order to enable applicant to appear before concerned Court, he is granted Transitory-cum-protective bail for a period of 10(Ten) days commencing from today, subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty thousand) and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. This order shall cease to have its effect on expiry of said period or on appearance of applicant before concerned Court, whichever is earlier. In case, applicant fails to surrender before the learned trial Court within stipulated period, his bail bond shall stand forfeited. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Ihsan/PS
